anchored_threads.thread_name: Sāṅkhya / first-principles knowledge (verses 2.11-2.30). What survives change; what is configuration vs. essence; the imperishable that is the substrate of action.
anchored_threads.anchor_evidence: The concessive structure of the verse rests on the architectural-invariant frame established in 2.20-2.25: the spec/implementation distinction is the engineering analog of the imperishable/body distinction. 2.26's contribution is the secular backup — even without the invariant, the conclusion holds.

progression_issue.explanation: The verse fits its position in the sāṅkhya block: 2.20-2.25 assert the imperishable; 2.26 grants the materialist premise as a hypothetical; 2.27 closes the hypothetical; 2.28-2.30 add independent arguments and the closing coda. The engineering layer respects this progression.
